devanagari: कालियस्य यमुनाह्रदे निवासस्य कारणवर्णनं । ह्रदान्निर्गतेन श्रीकृष्णेन व्रजौकसां दावानलाद् रक्षणम् । श्रीराजोवाच । नागालयं रमणकं कस्मात्...
english_devnagari: kāliyasya yamunāhrade nivāsasya kāraṇavarṇanaṃ hradānnirgatena śrīkṛṣṇena vrajaukasāṃ dāvānalād rakṣaṇam śrīrājovāca nāgālayaṃ ramaṇakaṃ kasmāt tatyāja kāliyaḥ kṛtaṃ kiṃ vā suparṇasya tenaikenāsamañjasam

translation:
King Parīkṣit enquired:
Why did Kāliya migrate from Ramaṇaka, the home of the Nāgas? Or what specific offence did he commit against Garuḍa?

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । उपहार्यैः सर्पजनैः मासि मासीह यो बलिः । वानस्पत्यो महाबाहो नागानां प्राङ्निरूपितः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca upahāryaiḥ sarpajanaiḥ māsi māsīha yo baliḥ vānaspatyo mahābāho nāgānāṃ prāṅnirūpitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka replied:
Oh King of mighty arms! It was formerly agreed[1] that whatever share of offerings was received by the Nāgas, every month, from the serpent-worshippers, was to be dedicated to the noble-souled Garuḍa, and to be deposited on every New Moon day at the foot of a particular tree (in Ramaṇaka) by serpents who were the prey of Garuḍa. All Nāgas thus contributed their share for self-protection.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
V.B.: quotes the following agreement brought about by Brahmā between Garuḍa and the serpents:
amāyāṃ vṛkṣa-mūle tu nāga-lokeṣu yad bhavet /
ekasmin divase tāvad ekatra sthāpayantu hi //
tato hi garuḍas tasmins tad bhuktvā naiva pīḍayet /
iti vyavasthavā sarpā māsi māsi baliṃ daduḥ //
SS. states that they offered one serpent per month to Garuḍa.

devanagari: स्वं स्वं भागं प्रयच्छन्ति नागाः पर्वणि पर्वणि । गोपीथायात्मनः सर्वे सुपर्णाय महात्मने ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: svaṃ svaṃ bhāgaṃ prayacchanti nāgāḥ parvaṇi parvaṇi gopīthāyātmanaḥ sarve suparṇāya mahātmane

translation:
Śrī Śuka replied:
Oh King of mighty arms! It was formerly agreed[1] that whatever share of offerings was received by the Nāgas, every month, from the serpent-worshippers, was to be dedicated to the noble-souled Garuḍa, and to be deposited on every New Moon day at the foot of a particular tree (in Ramaṇaka) by serpents who were the prey of Garuḍa. All Nāgas thus contributed their share for self-protection.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
V.B.: quotes the following agreement brought about by Brahmā between Garuḍa and the serpents:
amāyāṃ vṛkṣa-mūle tu nāga-lokeṣu yad bhavet /
ekasmin divase tāvad ekatra sthāpayantu hi //
tato hi garuḍas tasmins tad bhuktvā naiva pīḍayet /
iti vyavasthavā sarpā māsi māsi baliṃ daduḥ //
SS. states that they offered one serpent per month to Garuḍa.

devanagari: विषवीर्य मदाविष्टः काद्रवेयस्तु कालियः । कदर्थीकृत्य गरुडं स्वयं तं बुभुजे बलिम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣavīrya madāviṣṭaḥ kādraveyastu kāliyaḥ kadarthīkṛtya garuḍaṃ svayaṃ taṃ bubhuje balim

translation: Being elated with pride for its power and virulence of its poison, Kāliya, the son of Kadru, slighted Garuḍa, and ate up the offerings intended for him.

devanagari: तच्छ्रुत्वा कुपितो राजन् भगवान् भगवत्प्रियः । विजिघांसुर्महावेगः कालियं समुपाद्रवत् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tacchrutvā kupito rājan bhagavān bhagavatpriyaḥ vijighāṃsurmahāvegaḥ kāliyaṃ samupādravat

translation: On learning this, the glorious Garuḍa, beloved of the Lord, got enraged, and with a desire to kill Kāliya, swooped down on it with terrific speed, Oh King.

devanagari: तमापतन्तं तरसा विषायुधः । प्रत्यभ्ययाद् उच्छ्रितनैकमस्तकः । दद्भिः सुपर्णं व्यदशद् ददायुधः । कराल जिह्रोच्छ्वसितोग्र लोचनः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: tamāpatantaṃ tarasā viṣāyudhaḥ pratyabhyayād ucchritanaikamastakaḥ dadbhiḥ suparṇaṃ vyadaśad dadāyudhaḥ karāla jihrocchvasitogra locanaḥ
translation: Raising up its numerous heads (hoods), the serpent whose weapon is poison, advanced against Garuḍa who was vehemently rushing at it, and bit him with fangs as its weapons, and looked terrific with its dreadful tongues, poisonous hissing and cruel eyes.

devanagari: तं तार्क्ष्यपुत्रः स निरस्य मन्युमान् । प्रचण्डवेगो मधुसूदनासनः । पक्षेण सव्येन हिरण्यरोचिषा । जघान कद्रुसुतमुग्रविक्रमः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tārkṣyaputraḥ sa nirasya manyumān pracaṇḍavego madhusūdanāsanaḥ pakṣeṇa savyena hiraṇyarociṣā jaghāna kadrusutamugravikramaḥ

translation: Brushing it aside with the terrific vehemence of his speed, the wrathful Garuḍa, the son of Tārkṣya (the sage Kāśyapa), the carrier of Lord Viṣṇu and possessed of terrific prowess, struck it down with its left wing[2] brilliant like gold.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
VT. notes that it was to express his contempt for Kāliya that Garuḍa used his left wing.

devanagari: सुपर्णपक्षाभिहतः कालियोऽतीव विह्वलः । ह्रदं विवेश कालिन्द्याः तदगम्यं दुरासदम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: suparṇapakṣābhihataḥ kāliyo hradaṃ viveśa kālindyāḥ tadagamyaṃ durāsadam

translation: Struck down with the wing of Garuḍa, Kāliya became overwhelmed with fear and agitated. It entered the pool of the Yamunā that was out of bounds for Garuḍa, and hence unapproachable.

devanagari: तत्रैकदा जलचरं गरुडो भक्ष्यमीप्सितम् । निवारितः सौभरिणा प्रसह्य क्षुधितोऽहरत् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tatraikadā jalacaraṃ garuḍo bhakṣyamīpsitam nivāritaḥ saubhariṇā prasahya kṣudhito

translation: On one occasion Garuḍa, being extremely hungry carried away by force his favourite prey—an acquatic creature, though he was prohibited from doing so by the Sage Śaubhari.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Probably the same sage who married all the fifty daughters of Māndhātā. It might be during his stay in the pool of the Yamunā for performing the penance, that the above incident took place, (vide Supra) 9-6. 39-55, also Viṣṇu Purāṇa 4.2.69-131)

devanagari: मीनान् सुदुःखितान् दृष्ट्वा दीनान् मीनपतौ हते । कृपया सौभरिः प्राह तत्रत्यक्षेममाचरन् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: mīnān suduḥkhitān dṛṣṭvā dīnān mīnapatau hate kṛpayā saubhariḥ prāha tatratyakṣemamācaran

translation: Seeing that the fish were greatly over-whelmed with grief and felt miserable due to the death of that fish king, Śaubhari, out of compassion for the acquatic creatures in that pool, and for the safety of the creatures living in that pool declared;

devanagari: अत्र प्रविश्य गरुडो यदि मत्स्यान् स खादति । सद्यः प्राणैर्वियुज्येत सत्यमेतद् ब्रवीम्यहम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: atra praviśya garuḍo yadi matsyān sa khādati sadyaḥ prāṇairviyujyeta satyametad bravīmyaham
translation: “If ever Garuḍa enters this pool and eats the fish hereof, he will instantly meet death. I utter the truth.”

devanagari: तं कालियः परं वेद नान्यः कश्चन लेलिहः । अवात्सीद् गरुडाद् भीतः कृष्णेन च विवासितः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ kāliyaḥ paraṃ veda nānyaḥ kaścana lelihaḥ avātsīd garuḍād bhītaḥ kṛṣṇena ca vivāsitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Only Kāliya knew this (Śaubhari’s) curse but no other serpent knew it. So it resided in that pool out of the fear of Garuḍa. It was expelled out of it by Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: कृष्णं ह्रदाद् विनिष्क्रान्तं दिव्यस्रग् गन्धवाससम् । महामणिगणाकीर्णं जाम्बूनदपरिष्कृतम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇaṃ hradād viniṣkrāntaṃ divyasrag gandhavāsasam mahāmaṇigaṇākīrṇaṃ jāmbūnadapariṣkṛtam
translation: Finding Kṛsṇa come out of the pool, decorated with heavenly garlands, sandal pigment and rich dress and beautified with numerous costly big diamonds, gems and gold ornaments all over his Person, all cowherds leapt to their feet like sense-organs on return of life (or ‘creatures after the return of the vital Principle’), and with their hearts overwhelmed with rapturous joy, embraced him with deep affection.

devanagari: उपलभ्योत्थिताः सर्वे लब्धप्राणा इवासवः । प्रमोदनिभृतात्मानो गोपाः प्रीत्याभिरेभिरे ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: upalabhyotthitāḥ sarve labdhaprāṇā ivāsavaḥ pramodanibhṛtātmāno gopāḥ prītyābhirebhire
translation: Finding Kṛsṇa come out of the pool, decorated with heavenly garlands, sandal pigment and rich dress and beautified with numerous costly big diamonds, gems and gold ornaments all over his Person, all cowherds leapt to their feet like sense-organs on return of life (or ‘creatures after the return of the vital Principle’), and with their hearts overwhelmed with rapturous joy, embraced him with deep affection.

devanagari: यशोदा रोहिणी नन्दो गोप्यो गोपाश्च कौरव । कृष्णं समेत्य लब्धेहा आसन् लब्धमनोरथा ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: yaśodā rohiṇī nando gopyo gopāśca kaurava kṛṣṇaṃ sametya labdhehā āsan labdhamanorathā

translation: Regaining their consciousness, Yaśodā, Rohiṇī, Nanda and other cowherds and cowherdesses got their desires fulfilled by meeting Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: रामश्चाच्युतमालिङ्ग्य जहासास्यानुभाववित् । नगो गावो वृषा वत्सा लेभिरे परमां मुदम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: rāmaścācyutamāliṅgya jahāsāsyānubhāvavit nago gāvo vṛṣā vatsā lebhire paramāṃ mudam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Balarāma clasped Kṛṣṇa in the arms and laughed, as he knew the prowess of Kṛṣṇa.
[Bhāgavata Candrikā’s text adds this half-verse:]
16(A). He seated him on his lap and looked at him again and again in great affection. The trees, cows, bullocks, calves all were filled with extreme joy.

devanagari: नन्दं विप्राः समागत्य गुरवः सकलत्रकाः । ऊचुस्ते कालियग्रस्तो दिष्ट्या मुक्तस्तवात्मजः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: nandaṃ viprāḥ samāgatya guravaḥ sakalatrakāḥ ūcuste kāliyagrasto diṣṭyā muktastavātmajaḥ
translation: The Brāhmaṇa preceptors (of Nanda) along with their wives (who had already come there along with all the inhabitants of Vraja), now approached Nanda and congratulated him, “Thank God! How fortunate it is that your son seized by Kāliya is liberated from its clutches.

devanagari: देहि दानं द्विजातीनां कृष्णनिर्मुक्तिहेतवे । नन्दः प्रीतमना राजन् गाः सुवर्णं तदादिशत् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: dehi dānaṃ dvijātīnāṃ kṛṣṇanirmuktihetave nandaḥ prītamanā rājan gāḥ suvarṇaṃ tadādiśat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Confer some gifts on Brāhmaṇas, on account of the deliverance of Kṛṣṇa (from Kāliya)”. Delighted at heart Nanda immediately gave cows and gold as gifts.

devanagari: यशोदापि महाभागा नष्टलब्धप्रजा सती । परिष्वज्याङ्कमारोप्य मुमोचाश्रुकलां मुहुः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: yaśodāpi mahābhāgā naṣṭalabdhaprajā satī pariṣvajyāṅkamāropya mumocāśrukalāṃ muhuḥ

translation:
The highly fortunate Yaśodā who got restored to her child, which was as good as lost, hugged him. Seating him on her lap, he again and again shed tears of joy.
[Bhāgavata Candrikā’s text adds:]
19(A). In the meanwhile, when the sun set, Kṛṣṇa along with Rāma camped there along with their parents, cowherds and their wealth—the herds of cattle.

devanagari: तां रात्रिं तत्र राजेन्द्र क्षुत्तृड्भ्यां श्रमकर्षिताः । ऊषुर्व्रयौकसो गावः कालिन्द्या उपकूलतः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ rātriṃ tatra rājendra kṣuttṛḍbhyāṃ śramakarṣitāḥ ūṣurvrayaukaso gāvaḥ kālindyā upakūlataḥ

translation:
The inhabitants of Vraja and cows, oppressed as they were by hunger, thirst and physical exhaustion, passed that night near the bank of the Yamunā, oh King of Kings!
[Padaratnāvalī’s text adds: (a half verse)]
20(A). At that time, in Vraja, Oh best of Kurus!

devanagari: तदा शुचिवनोद्‍भूतो दावाग्निः सर्वतो व्रजम् । सुप्तं निशीथ आवृत्य प्रदग्धुमुपचक्रमे ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tadā śucivanod‍bhūto dāvāgniḥ sarvato vrajam suptaṃ niśītha āvṛtya pradagdhumupacakrame

translation: In the jungle dried up with summer, a great forest conflagration broke out at midnight, and surrounding the Vraja lying fast asleep, it began to scorch them.

devanagari: तत उत्थाय सम्भ्रान्ता दह्यमाना व्रजौकसः । कृष्णं ययुस्ते शरणं मायामनुजमीश्वरम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tata utthāya sambhrāntā dahyamānā vrajaukasaḥ kṛṣṇaṃ yayuste śaraṇaṃ māyāmanujamīśvaram

translation: Touched by the fire, the inhabitants of Vraja woke up and in their bewilderment sought resort to Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Lord who had assumed a human form through his Māyā.

devanagari: कृष्ण कृष्ण महाभाग हे रामामितविक्रम । एष घोरतमो वह्निः तावकान् ग्रसते हि नः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa mahābhāga he rāmāmitavikrama eṣa ghoratamo vahniḥ tāvakān grasate hi naḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: “Oh Kṛṣṇa! Oh highly fortunate Kṛṣṇa! Oh Rāma! Oh Rāma of infinite prowess! This extremely terrific fire is now consuming us who are yours (your devotees).

devanagari: सुदुस्तरान्नः स्वान् पाहि कालाग्नेः सुहृदः प्रभो । न शक्नुमः त्वच्चरणं संत्यक्तुं अकुतोभयम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: sudustarānnaḥ svān pāhi kālāgneḥ suhṛdaḥ prabho na śaknumaḥ tvaccaraṇaṃ saṃtyaktuṃ akutobhayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]

translation: Oh Lord! Protect us who are your well-wishers and friends, from this death-like (deadly) fire from which it is extremely difficult to escape. We cannot give up your feet (resorting to which) there is no fear from any quarter”.

devanagari: इत्थं स्वजनवैक्लव्यं निरीक्ष्य जगदीश्वरः । तं अग्निं अपिबत् तीव्रं अनंतोऽनन्त शक्तिधृक् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ svajanavaiklavyaṃ nirīkṣya jagadīśvaraḥ taṃ agniṃ apibat tīvraṃ anaṃto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Perceiving this helplessness and danger of his people (devotees), the Supreme Ruler of the earth, the Infinite Lord of unfathomable powers, swallowed up that terrible fire.
